title: Something Rumbles Underground
tagline: The ground is shaking. It's not Hercules this time. Zeus knows this and is trying not to show it.
arc_note: TITANS STIRRING — First background appearance. The Titan threat is introduced as a mystery, not a confrontation. Kids don't fully understand what it means yet. Zeus does, slightly. Athena does, fully.

characters.focus: Little Zeus — first time we see him worried about something he doesn't know how to fix
characters.threat: Underground tremors — not dangerous yet, but wrong. The Olympus Sea ripples without wind. The stone fish from EP003 vibrates. Old cracks appear in the mountain.

scenes.title: The Tremor
scenes.narration: The first tremor happened at breakfast. Not an earthquake — more like the mountain clearing its throat. The cups rattled. The Olympus Sea sent one wave in the wrong direction. Zeus felt it differently than the others. He put down his cup. He looked at the floor. He said: 'That wasn't Hercules.' Hercules, across the table, raised his hand to confirm. It wasn't him.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Breakfast table, cups rattling from a tremor. The group looking around. Zeus puts his cup down and looks at the floor with an expression nobody has seen on him before — not scared, something older. Hercules raises one enormous hand to confirm it wasn't him. In the background through the window, the Olympus Sea sends one wave inland. Wrong direction. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Old Cracks
scenes.narration: Athena found the cracks first — running along the east face of Olympus mountain, old stone newly stressed. She ran her hand along one. She tapped her chin. Three times, very slowly. She didn't say what she was thinking. Zeus found her there and asked. She said: 'Do you remember your father's stories about what's below the mountains?' Zeus said: 'He told a lot of stories.' She said: 'Yes.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena at the east face of Olympus mountain, tracing a crack with her finger. Three slow chin taps — the serious gesture, deliberate. Zeus arrives, reads her face. Her answer tells him more than the words do. Archie on her shoulder watches the crack. In the distance, another small tremor — dust falls from the cliff edge. 16:9.

scenes.title: Hercules Investigates
scenes.narration: Hercules decided to investigate by digging. He dug seventeen feet before Athena caught up to him. She said: 'Stop digging.' He said: 'I'm almost at the sound.' She said: 'That is exactly why you should stop.' He stopped. They stood at the edge of his hole. Something below — far, far below — vibrated once. Then stopped. Hercules looked at the hole. He said: 'Huh.' For Hercules, this counted as concern.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena at the edge of an enormous Hercules-dug hole, looking down at it with controlled concern. Hercules beside her looking down. From deep in the hole, a single vibration — visible as a ripple effect through the stone walls. Hercules says 'Huh.' with the expression of someone who has never said huh before. The hole is impressively deep. 16:9.

scenes.title: Zeus on the Mountain
scenes.narration: That evening, Zeus sat at the highest point of Olympus alone — above the clouds, where he went when something was important. Achilles found him there. He hadn't been invited. He sat down anyway. He said: 'What are you thinking about?' Zeus said: 'Old things.' Achilles said: 'Are old things dangerous?' Zeus said: 'Some of them.' He didn't say which. They sat there until the stars came out. No more tremors that night.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. QUIET SCENE — Zeus at the highest point of Olympus above the clouds, sitting, crown off, looking at the horizon. Achilles arrives and sits beside him without ceremony. Two kids, one serious conversation, enormous sky. Stars beginning. The tremors gone for now. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena's Note
scenes.narration: The next morning Athena left a note on Zeus's breakfast scroll. It said: We should ask your father about the deep ones. Not today. Not urgently. But we should ask. He read it twice. Then he wrote back: Yes. He left it on her chair. She found it and added: Good. Then she made a note in her master scroll — the first entry in a new section labeled WHAT LIVES BELOW.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Split — Zeus reading Athena's note with a quiet nod, then writing back. Athena finding his response and adding 'Good.' Then we see her scroll open to a new section, quill writing WHAT LIVES BELOW at the top of a fresh page. Archie watches the quill move. 16:9.

scenes.title: Today Was Fine
scenes.narration: The tremors didn't return that day. The mountain was quiet. Hercules refilled his hole (Athena asked). Perseus organized the fishing situation in the Olympus Sea. Achilles ran his circuits. The world was normal-sized again, for now. Zeus watched it all from the mountain and decided today was a good day. The note in Athena's scroll would wait. Not everything urgent needed to be today.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Montage of normal Olympus afternoon — Hercules filling the hole cheerfully, Perseus with the Olympus Sea dolphins, Achilles in mid-sprint sparkle trail, Zeus watching from above with a small smile, tension held but not heavy. The mountain solid beneath them all. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: Not every scary thing announces itself loudly. Sometimes it's just a cup rattling. Sometimes it's a crack in old stone. The brave thing isn't always running toward the noise — sometimes it's writing a note, making a plan, and deciding that today was good enough while you prepare for tomorrow. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — the group together at sunset, normal and warm. Athena's scroll in the background, the new section just started. Zeus's crown back on, slightly crooked. Everything fine, for now. The Olympus Sea calm. One dolphin visible. 16:9.

lesson: Not every problem needs to be solved today. Knowing something is there and making a plan is its own kind of courage.
titan_arc_note: FIRST appearance of Titan stirring. Introduced as mystery, not threat. WHAT LIVES BELOW scroll section begins. Zeus and Athena privately aligned. Next: EP015 (second stirring, more serious).
youtube_title: Something's Shaking Underground at Olympus... 😰 | Little Olympus Episode 10
next_episode_preview: Episode 11 — Hermes's Mix-Up! He delivered the messages. To the wrong people. All of them.
